title: How to Make Moroccan Preserved Lemons with Sea Salt
description: Moroccan preserved lemons are easy to make with just two ingredients: fresh lemons and sea salt. This salt-cured fermentation method produces intensely flavorful lemons perfect for adding to dishes, taking only 10 minutes to prepare and 4 weeks to cure.

ingredients.item: g fresh lemons (about 4 to 5 small lemons)

steps: Wash all of the lemons and weigh out the salt in a bowl.
Cut the ends off the lemons, then starting at one end, cut each lemon into quarters without cutting all the way through.
Over a large bowl, fill the crevices of the lemons with copious amounts of salt, filling each lemon with salt until all the salt is used up.
Start packing the whole lemons into a clean, large jar, wedging them in as tightly as possible until lemon juice is released in the process.
Once the jar is full, ensure all lemons are submerged in lemon juice; if needed, juice an extra lemon and add the juice to the jar.
Place a lid on the jar and allow the lemons to cure at room temperature for 4 weeks.

Once opened, store in the refrigerator indefinitely, rinsing off excess salt and brine before using the preserved lemons in recipes; discard the pulp and use the well-rinsed rind anywhere you would use lemon zest.
